challenges.title: Computed Style Properties
challenges.description: Write CSS that targets '#main-box' and sets display to flex, background-color to rgb(30, 60, 90), font-size to 18px, and padding to 20px. These properties would be accessible via JavaScript's getComputedStyle(). Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '#main-box' in css.lower(), 'Should target #main-box'
assert 'display' in css.lower(), 'Should set display property'
assert 'flex' in css.lower(), 'Should use display: flex'
assert 'background-color' in css.lower(), 'Should set background-color'
assert 'rgb(' in css.lower(), 'Should use rgb() color value'
assert 'font-size' in css.lower(), 'Should set font-size'
assert '18px' in css, 'Should use 18px font-size'
assert 'padding' in css.lower(), 'Should set padding'
assert '20px' in css, 'Should use 20px padding'
hints: The CSSOM (CSS Object Model) allows JavaScript to read and manipulate styles programmatically.
getComputedStyle() returns the resolved values of all CSS properties on an element.
Write standard CSS rules — the CSSOM reads whatever styles are applied.

challenges.title: Custom Properties for Programmatic Access
challenges.description: Write CSS that defines custom properties on :root for --primary-color (set to #3498db), --spacing-unit (set to 8px), and --font-main (set to a sans-serif font stack). Then target '.widget' and use all three custom properties via var(): set color to --primary-color, padding to --spacing-unit, and font-family to --font-main. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert ':root' in css.lower(), 'Should define custom properties on :root'
assert '--primary-color' in css.lower(), 'Should define --primary-color'
assert '--spacing-unit' in css.lower(), 'Should define --spacing-unit'
assert '--font-main' in css.lower(), 'Should define --font-main'
assert '#3498db' in css.lower(), 'Should set --primary-color to #3498db'
assert '8px' in css, 'Should set --spacing-unit to 8px'
assert 'var(' in css.lower(), 'Should use var() to reference custom properties'
assert '.widget' in css.lower(), 'Should target .widget'
assert 'sans-serif' in css.lower(), 'Should include sans-serif in font stack'
assert 'color' in css.lower(), 'Should set color using custom property'
assert 'padding' in css.lower(), 'Should set padding using custom property'
assert 'font-family' in css.lower(), 'Should set font-family using custom property'
hints: Custom properties (CSS variables) are accessible via the CSSOM using element.style.getPropertyValue('--name').
Define variables on :root and consume them with var(--variable-name).
A font stack like 'Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif' provides fallbacks.
